intent.feature_concept: Reusable headless UI primitives layer (Dropdown/popover, Modal, Toast) plus a project-owned inline SVG Icon component for an Electron+React desktop app; styled via design tokens, build-vs-buy evaluated against headless libraries
intent.topic: Reusable headless UI primitives layer (Dropdown/popover, Modal, Toast) plus a project-owned inline SVG Icon component for an Electron+React desktop app; styled via design tokens, build-vs-buy evaluated against headless libraries
intent.scope_summary: A reusable presentation-only primitives layer for the Electron+React renderer, delivering four user-facing components plus their shared overlay substrate. Components: (1) Dropdown/popover anchored to a trigger — keyboard navigation, focus return, click-outside + Escape dismiss, edge-aware positioning; (2) Modal dialog — focus trap, scrim/backdrop, Escape close, body scroll lock; (3) transient Toast — stacked, auto + manual dismiss, hover-pause; (4) inline SVG Icon driven by a project-owned ~40-icon set (1.5px stroke, 16x16 viewBox). First-class shared substrate (in lib/): a Portal, a focus-trap/focus-return hook, a click-outside hook, centralized Escape/keyboard handling, and an edge-aware positioning helper — reusable by future primitives. An explicit build-vs-buy evaluation (headless libs Radix/React Aria/Ark/Headless UI vs hand-rolled) governs the overlay substrate. Styling via semantic class names mapped to design/tokens.json; visual/behavior reference is the design export, never its markup.
intent.verbatim_prompt: A reusable UI primitives layer for a desktop app: a Dropdown / popover anchored to a trigger element (keyboard navigation, focus return, click-outside and Escape dismiss, edge-aware positioning), a Modal dialog (focus trap, scrim, Escape close), and a transient Toast. These overlay/focus/keyboard primitives are exactly the area where hand-rolled implementations accumulate accessibility and edge-case bugs, so EVALUATE HEADLESS PRIMITIVE LIBRARIES (e.g. Radix UI, React Aria, Ark UI, Headless UI) against a hand-rolled implementation as an explicit build-vs-buy decision — styling is supplied separately via design tokens, so a headless (unstyled) approach is viable and likely preferred for the overlay primitives. Separately, an inline SVG Icon component driven by a small project-owned icon set (~40 icons, 1.5px stroke, 16x16 viewBox) — the icon set already exists as hand-authored SVG paths, so keep it project-owned rather than pulling an icon dependency. All presentation-only; no request/response/persistence logic. Visual reference: design/reference.html (a Claude Design export) — look and behavior only, never reproduce its markup; it carries generated cruft (data-om-* attributes, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, a tweaks-panel) that must not enter the build. Whatever the build-vs-buy outcome, style with semantic class names from each component's role and design/tokens.json, not inline styles.

constraints.content: Presentation-only — no request/response/persistence logic. Styling exclusively via semantic class names (from each component's role) + design/tokens.json; no inline styles. Target stack: Electron renderer process, React 19, TypeScript, electron-vite. Accessibility: WCAG keyboard + focus expectations (focus trap/return, escape, ARIA roles). Dependency policy: a well-maintained headless library (Radix/React Aria/Ark) is acceptable as a runtime dep where it wins on a11y/edge-case correctness — bundle size a minor concern for desktop Electron; build-vs-buy decided per-primitive. Icon stays project-owned (no icon dependency). design/reference.html consulted for look/behavior only; its generated cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel) must never enter the build.

Out of scope: Beyond scope: (1) any network, request/response, or persistence logic — this set does no fetching or storing; (2) extra widgets beyond those named here (no buttons, fields, tabs, tables, and so on); (3) a theming engine, runtime theme switcher, or authoring of new style files.
§4 Always (project): shared renderer state in zustand + Never mutate outside store actions

affected_areas.impact: greenfield — new atoms/ (Icon) + molecules/ (Dropdown/Modal/Toast) subdirs; grounded by existing components/Versions.tsx
new lib/ subdir for Portal + focus-trap/click-outside/positioning hooks; grounded by App.tsx/main.tsx prefix
design tokens ALREADY compiled to CSS custom properties here; semantic-class styling via these CSS vars is the established wiring
unverified path — NO test framework in package.json; automated interaction tests (success_criteria) require adding a runner + RTL/dom
@renderer -> src/renderer/src alias already configured; primitives import surface ready

risks.risk: Spike: render a Radix Dialog + DropdownMenu styled SOLELY via semantic classes bound to tokens.css CSS vars — confirm zero inline styles and a look matching the design reference
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Stand up the renderer test stack (Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event + jsdom/happy-dom) and write one keyboard/focus interaction test as a smoke proof; record the stack in docs/architecture.md per constitution 3.4
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Verify Radix Toast covers stacking + hover/focus pause + rapid-fire queue; if not, scope the small project-owned zustand toast queue instead
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Prototype nested-overlay behavior (modal opens a dropdown; toast over modal) to confirm Escape closes topmost-only and focus-trap/return compose correctly
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Author the Icon contract: derive the typed name union from the ~40 SVG paths, define size/stroke props and the unknown-name fallback, confirm prefers-reduced-motion handling
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: no test framework — must add vitest + @testing-library/react + jsdom/happy-dom (or Playwright component tests)

open_questions.question: user-supplied mechanism guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): keep Icon project-owned rather than pulling an icon dependency (icon set already exists as hand-authored SVG paths)

design_options.name: Radix primitives + token-styled wrappers
design_options.shape: Thin project-owned PascalCase components wrap Radix Dialog/DropdownMenu/Popover/Toast; behavior, ARIA, focus-trap/return, keyboard nav, and edge-aware positioning come from Radix (official React 19 support). Styling supplied only as semantic class names bound to the existing tokens.css CSS custom properties. Icon stays fully project-owned (typed name union over the ~40 SVG paths). Toast queue lives in a zustand store driving an imperative toast(msg,opts) API.
pros: Least code; the exact a11y/edge-case bug class the user flagged is offloaded to a battle-tested lib
Radix Dialog/Dropdown/Popover are stable + React 19 supported; only complex combobox/multiselect lagged (not needed)
Styling stays project-owned via semantic classes; no inline styles, fits tokens.css wiring directly
cons: Runtime dependency on Radix (acceptable per dep policy)
Must learn Radix data-attribute/state hooks to style states (open/closed, side)
Some bundle weight (minor for desktop Electron)

design_options.name: Hand-rolled on Floating UI + native dialog
design_options.shape: Project-owned components with minimal deps: Modal built on native <dialog> (free focus trap, scrim, Escape, top-layer); Dropdown/popover positioned by Floating UI (flip/shift, ~3kB) with FloatingFocusManager; Toast via a zustand queue + Portal. Shared lib/ hooks (useClickOutside, useFocusReturn, useKeyboardNav) supply the rest. One small positioning dep.
pros: Minimal dependency surface; full source ownership
Leverages the platform — single known Chromium makes <dialog>/Popover-API/top-layer reliable
Floating UI is version-agnostic and tiny
cons: Re-incurs the exact a11y/edge-case risk the user called out: menu typeahead, roving tabindex, nested focus-trap composition, ARIA wiring
Toast stacking/hover-pause + nested-overlay Escape ordering are all hand-built

design_options.name: shadcn-style copy-in over Radix
design_options.shape: Copy Radix-based component source (shadcn-style) directly into components/, own and restyle it with token CSS vars rather than depending on a wrapper library. Behavior is Radix's, but the source lives in-repo with no release-cadence coupling. Icon + Toast store remain project-authored.
pros: Full styling control; no opaque wrapper layer
No runtime coupling to a wrapper lib release cadence
cons: More component code to maintain in-repo
Must manually track upstream a11y/security fixes
Still carries the underlying Radix dependency

build_vs_buy.build_path: Build project-owned: the Icon component (typed name union over the ~40 hand-authored SVG paths, unknown-name fallback), all styling (semantic class names -> tokens.css CSS vars), the Toast queue (zustand store + imperative toast() API), and thin wrappers/composition around the bought behavior.
build_vs_buy.buy_path: Adopt Radix Primitives for the overlay behavior/a11y of Dropdown/popover, Modal (Dialog), and Toast — focus trap/return, keyboard nav, ARIA roles, edge-aware positioning, light-dismiss — restyled via semantic classes. (Alternatives if Radix is rejected: React Aria for deepest a11y, or Floating UI for a hand-rolled positioning substrate.)
build_vs_buy.reasoning: Buy the behavior that is expensive and risky to get right (the overlay/focus/keyboard correctness the user flagged), build the parts that are cheap, project-specific, and dependency-free (Icon, styling, Toast queue). Pure build re-incurs the flagged bug class; pure buy would fight the token-styled, project-owned, no-inline-styles requirement. Hybrid satisfies both.

cited_canonical_patterns.relevance: Mature unstyled Dialog/DropdownMenu/Popover/Toast with full a11y, keyboard, focus mgmt, RTL; official React 19 support. WorkOS-acquired — velocity slowed for COMPLEX comps (combobox/multiselect, not needed here); Dialog/Dropdown/Popover stable. Heavy prod usage via shadcn.
Deepest a11y primitives available (ARIA APG-compliant), Modal/Popover/Menu/Toast; more code per component, unstyled. Strongest correctness bar.
Emerging primitive layer, now the more actively-maintained Radix alternative; Dialog/Popover/Menu unstyled. Newer = smaller battle-test surface.
Cross-framework headless primitives backed by XState machines; predictable complex-component behavior. Heavier abstraction than needed for app-internal React-only layer.
~10 unstyled comps incl. Dialog/Menu/Popover; simplest API but Tailwind-oriented and no Toast. Smallest scope.

Best low-level positioning (~3kB, edge-aware flip/shift, virtual elements) + modal/non-modal focus trapping. Used UNDER THE HOOD by Radix/Base UI. The realistic substrate for a hand-rolled overlay path.

cited_canonical_patterns.reference: Native Popover API + dialog element + CSS anchor positioning
cited_canonical_patterns.relevance: Electron bundles a single known Chromium → no browser-matrix concern; <dialog> gives free focus trap + scrim + Escape, Popover API gives top-layer + light-dismiss. Anchor-positioning availability is Chromium-version-gated (derisk).

Dominant consumption pattern: copy component source (built on Radix) into the repo, own + restyle it. Project-owned source with battle-tested behavior — directly relevant to a token-styled, project-owned layer.

plan_seeds.recommended_option_rationale: Best matches the stated intent and constraints. The user explicitly framed overlay/focus/keyboard as the bug-prone area and the dep policy permits a well-maintained headless library — so offloading that behavior to Radix (stable Dialog/Dropdown/Popover/Toast, React 19 supported) buys the most correctness for the least code, while semantic-class styling against the existing tokens.css keeps presentation fully project-owned. Icon stays project-owned (no icon dependency) since the SVG set already exists. Tradeoffs acknowledged: a Radix runtime dep and learning its state/data-attribute styling hooks; if a zero-overlay-dependency rule ever emerges, fall back to 'Hand-rolled on Floating UI + native dialog', or choose 'shadcn-style copy-in over Radix' if in-repo source ownership outweighs the maintenance cost.

discovery_block.fit_rationale: Strong structural fit: greenfield renderer with React 19 + TS + a configured @renderer alias, and design tokens already compiled to CSS custom properties (src/renderer/styles/tokens.css) — exactly the semantic-class styling channel the spec wants, so styling needs no new infrastructure. The atomic-design dirs (atoms/molecules/lib) are clean additions. The one non-trivial gap is test tooling: the automated-interaction-test success criterion has no runner/library installed, so a vitest + @testing-library setup task is required (Medium effort). Both user mechanism guesses hold up: headless-lib-preferred is reasonable (Radix/Base UI/React Aria all support React 19) BUT a native <dialog>+Popover-API+Floating-UI hybrid is a real contender given Electron's single Chromium; Icon-project-owned is sound since the icon set already exists and needs no dependency.

fit_assessments.reality: greenfield: only components/Versions.tsx exists; clean to add atoms/ + molecules/

fit_assessments.user_expected: shared overlay/focus/keyboard hooks + positioning helper in lib/
fit_assessments.reality: no lib/ dir yet; clean greenfield add

fit_assessments.user_expected: style via design/tokens.json semantic class names, no inline styles
fit_assessments.reality: tokens already emitted as CSS custom properties in src/renderer/styles/tokens.css — semantic-class approach is the existing pattern; strong fit

fit_assessments.user_expected: automated keyboard/focus/dismiss interaction tests gate the feature
fit_assessments.reality: no test runner or component-testing library installed at all
blockers: no test framework — must add vitest + @testing-library/react + jsdom/happy-dom (or Playwright component tests)

fit_assessments.user_expected: primitives importable as a reusable internal layer

functional_scope.value: A reusable presentation-only primitives layer for the Electron+React renderer, delivering four user-facing components plus their shared overlay substrate. Components: (1) Dropdown/popover anchored to a trigger — keyboard navigation, focus return, click-outside + Escape dismiss, edge-aware positioning; (2) Modal dialog — focus trap, scrim/backdrop, Escape close, body scroll lock; (3) transient Toast — stacked, auto + manual dismiss, hover-pause; (4) inline SVG Icon driven by a project-owned ~40-icon set (1.5px stroke, 16x16 viewBox). First-class shared substrate (in lib/): a Portal, a focus-trap/focus-return hook, a click-outside hook, centralized Escape/keyboard handling, and an edge-aware positioning helper — reusable by future primitives. An explicit build-vs-buy evaluation (headless libs Radix/React Aria/Ark/Headless UI vs hand-rolled) governs the overlay substrate. Styling via semantic class names mapped to design/tokens.json; visual/behavior reference is the design export, never its markup.

users.value: App-internal only. Direct consumers are mintEnvoy renderer feature components (and the developers wiring them) — these primitives are an internal building-block layer, not a published package. End-users of the desktop app interact with the rendered overlays (dropdown menus, modals, toasts) and icons. No external-consumer API-stability concern.

inputs_outputs.value: All inputs are component props / imperative calls; all outputs are rendered DOM + callbacks. No external/network data crosses the boundary. Toast: imperative API (e.g. toast(message, opts)) backed by a provider/context that owns the live toast queue; outputs are stacked transient notifications + auto/manual dismiss callbacks. Dropdown/popover + Modal: controlled (open + onOpenChange props) — caller owns open state; inputs include trigger ref/element, menu items or children, placement hints; outputs are select/close callbacks and focus-return side effects. Icon: props are name (key into the project-owned SVG path set), size, and className; output is an inline <svg>. Styling inputs come only from semantic class names mapped to design tokens.

integration_points.value: Renderer process only (src/renderer/src). Atomic-design layout: Icon in src/renderer/src/components/atoms/; Dropdown/popover, Modal, Toast in src/renderer/src/components/molecules/; shared overlay/focus/keyboard hooks and positioning helpers in src/renderer/src/lib/. Styling sourced from design/tokens.json via semantic class names (likely CSS module or token-driven stylesheet under the same dirs). No main/preload-process involvement. Existing renderer is electron-vite boilerplate (App.tsx, components/Versions.tsx, main.tsx) — greenfield for primitives. NOTE: two user mechanism guesses still to reconcile in Phase 2 fit-check — (a) headless lib 'likely preferred' for overlays, (b) Icon kept project-owned.

constraints.value: Presentation-only — no request/response/persistence logic. Styling exclusively via semantic class names (from each component's role) + design/tokens.json; no inline styles. Target stack: Electron renderer process, React 19, TypeScript, electron-vite. Accessibility: WCAG keyboard + focus expectations (focus trap/return, escape, ARIA roles). Dependency policy: a well-maintained headless library (Radix/React Aria/Ark) is acceptable as a runtime dep where it wins on a11y/edge-case correctness — bundle size a minor concern for desktop Electron; build-vs-buy decided per-primitive. Icon stays project-owned (no icon dependency). design/reference.html consulted for look/behavior only; its generated cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel) must never enter the build.

non_goals.value: Beyond scope: (1) any network, request/response, or persistence logic — this set does no fetching or storing; (2) extra widgets beyond those named here (no buttons, fields, tabs, tables, and so on); (3) a theming engine, runtime theme switcher, or authoring of new style files.

success_criteria.value: All four primitives (Dropdown/popover, Modal, Toast, Icon) render per the visual reference look and behave correctly: keyboard nav, focus trap + focus return, click-outside + Escape dismiss, edge-aware positioning, scrim, toast auto-dismiss — meeting WCAG keyboard/focus expectations. Gated by automated interaction tests (keyboard/focus/dismiss). A demo/gallery harness exercises every primitive in its states as the visual + manual-QA surface.

edge_cases.value: Dropdown/popover: edge-aware flip/shift to stay within viewport (small Electron windows), overflowing content scrolls; trigger unmount while open. Modal + nested overlays: stacked overlays (modal->dropdown, toast over modal) compose — Escape closes the topmost overlay, focus trap + focus-return nest correctly, scrim/z-order sane, body scroll lock. Toast: multiple stack and auto-dismiss independently, rapid-fire queue, hover/focus pauses timer, manual dismiss. Keyboard: Tab wrap inside trap, arrow keys + Home/End in menu, return focus to trigger on close. Icon: safe fallback for unknown name; every animation respects prefers-reduced-motion.

references: design/reference.html (Claude Design export — look/behavior reference only)

gaps.description: user-supplied mechanism guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): keep Icon project-owned rather than pulling an icon dependency (icon set already exists as hand-authored SVG paths)
